title: tumalis bojeri
intro: Tumalis bojeri, commonly known as the "tumalis" or "wild camphor", is a species of plant in the family Rutaceae. This plant is known for its medicinal properties and health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Tumalis bojeri include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of this plant include camphor, cineole, and borneol, which exhibit therapeutic actions such as analgesia, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties.
side_effects: Tumalis bojeri can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing allergic reactions, which may manifest as hives, itching, or difficulty breathing. Prolonged consumption of the plant can also lead to gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Furthermore, excessive use of tumalis bojeri may cause cardiovascular problems, including increased heart rate and blood pressure. In severe cases, improper use of the plant can even result in seizures or coma.
